top_signals.title: Oil Keeps Flowing Through Hormuz Despite Iran Saying It’s Shut
top_signals.summary: Millions of barrels of oil continued to flow through the Strait of Hormuz this weekend even after Iran claimed to have closed the waterway again, as Washington and Tehran offer contrasting narratives over the status of the world’s most important shipping chokepoint.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Iran to close Strait of Hormuz yet Trump threatens toll
top_signals.summary: Iran’s top military command has said it will shut the Strait of Hormuz in response to alleged violations of the ceasefire agreement though President Trump threatened to impose a toll if the stretch of water is closed off. The Iranian leadership said the crucial shipping passage will be closed to vessel traffic as the “first [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon on the new world of AI agents
top_signals.summary: CNBC's Arjun Kharpal sits down with the chip company chief on The Tech Download podcast.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
